input.title: Function Redefinition
input.error_message:
main.cpp: In function ‘void calculate_0()’:
main.cpp:7:6: error: redefinition of ‘void calculate_0()’
    7 | void calculate_0() {
      |      ^~~~~
main.cpp:3:6: note: ‘void calculate_0()’ previously defined here

output.technical: The function `calculate_0()` is defined multiple times, violating the One Definition Rule (ODR).
output.beginner: You wrote two functions with the exact same name `calculate_0`. The compiler doesn't know which one to use.
output.analogy: It's like having two houses with the exact same address on the same street. The mail carrier is confused.
output.solution: Remove the duplicate function definition or rename one of them.
output.prevention: Keep function names unique within the same namespace.
